initial_setup_wizard_subtitle: This wizard will guide you through the initial configuration of your Defguard instance.
initial_setup_welcome_title: Welcome to Defguard initial configuration wizard.
initial_setup_welcome_subtitle: This wizard walks you through the steps to configure your VPN connection with a simple and intuitive setup process.
initial_setup_login_subtitle: Please enter your admin credentials to continue the setup wizard.
initial_setup_step_admin_user_descriptio: Manage core details and connection parameters for your VPN location.
initial_setup_step_general_config_descri: Manage core details and connection parameters for your VPN location.
initial_setup_step_internal_url_settings: Configure the internal URL and SSL settings for your Defguard instance.
initial_setup_step_external_url_settings: Configure the publicly accessible URL for the Edge component.
initial_setup_step_edge_deploy_descripti: Before we can adopt an Edge, this component needs to be launched.
initial_setup_step_edge_component_descri: Set up your VPN proxy quickly and ensure secure, optimized traffic flow for your users.
initial_setup_step_edge_adoption_descrip: Review the system’s checks and see if any issues need attention before deployment.
initial_setup_step_confirmation_descript: Your configuration was successful. You’re all set.
initial_setup_admin_user_password_rule_m: Password must be at least 8 characters
initial_setup_admin_user_password_rule_n: Password must contain at least one number
initial_setup_admin_user_password_rule_s: Password must contain at least one special character
initial_setup_admin_user_password_rule_l: Password must contain at least one lowercase letter
initial_setup_admin_user_password_rule_u: Password must contain at least one uppercase letter
initial_setup_admin_user_error_username_: Username must be at least 3 characters
initial_setup_admin_user_error_create_fa: Failed to create admin user. Please try again.
initial_setup_general_config_error_defgu: Defguard URL must use a hostname, not an IP address
initial_setup_general_config_error_admin: Default admin group name is required
initial_setup_general_config_error_auth_: Authentication period must be at least 1 day
initial_setup_general_config_error_mfa_t: MFA code timeout must be at least 60 seconds
initial_setup_general_config_error_save_: Failed to save settings. Please try again.
initial_setup_general_config_error_publi: Public Proxy URL must be a valid URL
initial_setup_auto_adoption_wizard_subti: Complete the final three steps to fully configure Defguard.
initial_setup_auto_adoption_welcome_subt: Unfortunately, the automated setup for some components did not complete successfully. Find detailed errors below.
We have successfully configured all the necessary components (gateway and edge) using Docker for this instance. Now, we need to configure some general settings.
initial_setup_auto_adoption_success_guid: This guide will walk you through the process.

If you would like to understand some basic Defguard concepts, each screen includes links to documentation as well as short videos with explanations that you can watch directly during the setup process.
initial_setup_auto_adoption_failed_suppo: If you are a Business or Enterprise customer, please
and provide the logs you see in the error summary section above.
If you are an Open Source or Free plan user, find support on
initial_setup_auto_adoption_step_admin_u: Enter your credentials of the first admin user.
initial_setup_auto_adoption_step_externa: Configure the publicly accessible URL for the Edge component.
initial_setup_auto_adoption_step_vpn_set: First VPN Location Public and Internal Settings
initial_setup_auto_adoption_step_mfa_set: You can enable Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for your VPN.
initial_setup_auto_adoption_step_summary: Everything is set up and ready to go!
initial_setup_auto_adoption_mfa_option_i: Uses the MFA methods configured in your Defguard profile.
After completing the initial DefGuard setup, configure MFA in your profile to enable it.
initial_setup_auto_adoption_mfa_option_e: Requires configuring an external identity provider in the settings, such as Google, Microsoft Entra ID, Okta, or JumpCloud.
initial_setup_auto_adoption_url_settings: This URL will be used to access and control Defguard. It should not be exposed to the Internet, only to the internal or VPN network. Below, you can also configure how to secure it with HTTPS (via Defguard or an external reverse proxy).

The Edge component is meant to be exposed publicly to the Internet. It handles various tasks, such as enabling automated user enrollment and sending automated configuration updates to desktop and mobile clients. It requires a dedicated URL and must be publicly accessible on the Internet. You can change the public URL later in General Settings.
initial_setup_auto_adoption_internal_url: This URL will be used to access and control Defguard. It should not be exposed to the Internet, only to the internal or VPN network. Below, you can also configure how to secure it with HTTPS (via Defguard or an external reverse proxy).
You will manage SSL termination yourself, for example via a reverse proxy.
Generate certificates based on Defguard internal CA
Defguard will generate and manage a certificate signed by its internal CA.
Provide your own certificate and private key files.
Please upload both a certificate and a key file before continuing.
You have opted to deploy a custom Reverse Proxy and manage your own SSL certificates. Please ensure your proxy configuration correctly routes traffic to the:
Defguard Edge service via TCP port 8080
You have chosen to secure Core with Defguard's internal CA, here you can download the CA Certificate Root File, please import it in your browser.

Your uploaded Certificate has been successfully validated. All required files were checked and confirmed as correct and ready for use. You can download the validated certificate files if needed for your setup.
initial_setup_auto_adoption_external_url: The Edge component is meant to be exposed publicly to the Internet. It handles various tasks, such as enabling automated user enrollment and sending automated configuration updates to desktop and mobile clients. It requires a dedicated URL and must be publicly accessible on the Internet. You can change the public URL later in General Settings.

The default Edge component port is 8080. If you choose not to configure SSL, please point your reverse proxy to this port. If you do configure SSL, you can access Edge via the standard 443 (HTTPS) port on the server where Edge is running.
You will manage SSL termination yourself, for example via a reverse proxy. Point your reverse proxy to 8080 on this host.
Defguard will automatically obtain and renew a certificate from Let's Encrypt for the domain in the URL. Please remember that ports 80 and 443 must point to this host and be publicly accessible.
Generate certificates based on Defguard internal CA
Defguard will generate and manage a certificate signed by its internal CA.
Provide your own certificate and private key files.
Please upload both a certificate and a key file before continuing.
You have opted to deploy a custom Reverse Proxy and manage your own SSL certificates. Please ensure your proxy configuration correctly routes traffic to the:
Defguard Core service via TCP port 8000
Validating with Let's Encrypt the domain name
You have chosen to secure the Edge component with Defguard's internal CA, here you can download the CA Certificate Root File, please import it in your browser.

Your uploaded Certificate has been successfully validated. All required files were checked and confirmed as correct and ready for use. You can download the validated certificate files if needed for your setup.
The Defguard Edge component handles SSL termination for the external URL automatically. Your public Edge URL is secured with HTTPS using a certificate managed by the Edge component.
initial_setup_auto_adoption_vpn_intro: To make the VPN operational, a few basic parameters must be configured. WireGuard® needs to be publicly accessible on a specific IP address and UDP port. This IP does not have to be set directly on the gateway it can be configured on your firewall or router and forwarded to the Defguard Gateway.
initial_setup_auto_adoption_vpn_helper_p: This IP or domain address must be public, as clients will use it to connect to the VPN. It does not need to be configured on the host where you deployed Defguard; we need the address because it will be distributed to all clients in their configuration files.
initial_setup_auto_adoption_vpn_helper_w: We will run the VPN Gateway on this port. If the host where Defguard is running is not publicly accessible, please forward this port from the IP address set as the VPN WireGuard Port to the internal IP address and this port.
initial_setup_auto_adoption_vpn_gateway_: We need to configure the internal VPN network. Set the IP address/mask for the gateway server, and we will configure the VPN network based on it. You can configure multiple addresses separated by commas.
initial_setup_auto_adoption_vpn_helper_g: The gateway IP address must be in CIDR format: IP/mask (e.g., 10.10.0.1/24). Based on this address, we will configure the VPN network (e.g., 10.10.0.0/24). You can define multiple IPv4 and IPv6 addresses (networks) separated by commas.
initial_setup_auto_adoption_vpn_allowed_: If you want your local networks to be accessible from VPN, list them in CIDR addresses/masks format below, separated by commas.
initial_setup_auto_adoption_vpn_helper_a: The defined networks (e.g., 10.10.0.0/24, fd00::/8) will be made available to clients connecting to the VPN.
initial_setup_auto_adoption_vpn_dns_desc: Configure (optionally) a custom DNS server for VPN connections (e.g., your local network DNS or a preferred DNS to use while connected to the VPN).
initial_setup_auto_adoption_summary_erro: Timed out waiting for settings essentials.
initial_setup_auto_adoption_summary_note: Please note that if the host running Defguard is not publicly accessible (i.e., it does not have the VPN public IP assigned to it), you must forward the following ports to it:
initial_setup_auto_adoption_summary_docs: Get familiar with our security concepts and architecture
initial_setup_auto_adoption_summary_comm: Join our community and participate in discussion
initial_setup_ca_error_validity_min: Validity period must be at least 1 year
initial_setup_ca_error_create_failed: Failed to create CA. Please review the information and try again.
initial_setup_ca_error_upload_failed: Failed to upload CA. Please ensure the certificate file is valid and try again.
initial_setup_ca_option_create_title: Create a certificate authority & configure all Defguard components
initial_setup_ca_option_create_descripti: By choosing this option, Defguard will create its own certificate authority and automatically configure all components to use its certificates — no manual setup required.
initial_setup_ca_helper_common_name: Can be any name you wish.
initial_setup_ca_helper_email: Each certificate authority (or any certificate) has a contact email field. Can be your email or general IT email.
initial_setup_ca_option_use_own_descript: Upload your certificate authority certificate and Defguard will use it to issue and configure certificates for components.
initial_setup_ca_generated_subtitle: The system created all required certificate files, including the root certificate and private key. You can download these files and continue with the configuration.
initial_setup_ca_validated_subtitle: Your uploaded Certificate Authority has been successfully validated. All required files were checked and confirmed as correct and ready for use. You can download the validated CA files if needed for your setup.
initial_setup_confirmation_error_finish_: Failed to finish setup. Please try again.
initial_setup_confirmation_lead: You've completed the first stage of the setup. Defguard is almost ready to go.
initial_setup_confirmation_title: In order to fully deploy Defguard you need:
initial_setup_confirmation_action_subtit: To organize users, manage access, track users activity and device monitoring.
initial_setup_confirmation_footer: Once you create your first location, the only step left will be to connect a gateway — and the system will be fully ready to use. This usually takes about n 10–15 minutes, depending on the complexity of your VPN configuration.
initial_setup_wizard_welcome_docs_descri: Before installation, we recommend reading our documentation to understand the system architecture and core components.
initial_setup_wizard_footer_support_text: For help, contact our support team at
